name: Search Quality Test Suite
purpose: Catch weaknesses, verify fixes on the same examples, and prevent regressions across suite guardrails, real short queries, clinical coverage, cycling regression, long documents, consumer lay language, workflow intent, core source-specific evidence, and precision safety.

layers.weakness_found: Code-level regressions can invalidate benchmark results before the live API is tested.
layers.recommended_solution: Fix failing unit/static tests before interpreting live search benchmark output.

layers.weakness_found: The current search-quality suite is driven by reviewed failures and focused probes. A green suite can still overstate broad clinical coverage if the expected CUIs are concentrated in a few semantic groups or workflows.
layers.recommended_solution: Track expected and disallowed CUI coverage by semantic group and source file, then add prevalence- or workflow-weighted common clinical concepts as a separate benchmark layer instead of relying only on one-off failures.

layers.weakness_found: Simple high-demand searches can regress even when paragraph recall looks acceptable.
layers.recommended_solution: Keep reviewed real short queries as a strict release lane, with broad terms represented by one primary CUI plus acceptable alternatives.

layers.weakness_found: The current 168-example suite still has 17 examples missing at least one expected concept at top 10.
layers.recommended_solution: Treat this as the pinned broad baseline and work the 17 misses without allowing the top-10 counts to regress.

layers.weakness_found: A fixed smoke set can hide regressions; cycling forces each iteration to cover a different slice.
layers.recommended_solution: Keep changing the rotation seed for every suite run and promote recurring misses into targeted tests.

layers.weakness_found: The core clinical benchmark is strong but still underrepresents realistic note formats such as discharge summaries, radiology reports, pathology reports, operative notes, nursing notes, lab flowsheets, medication administration records, therapy notes, home health notes, telephone triage, consult notes, and prior authorization text. First live baseline: 5/12 rows fully found at top 10, 52/60 expected concepts found, 7 rows missing at least one expected concept, and 0 wrong first results.
layers.recommended_solution: Work the missing secondary concepts in the realistic note-format probe, then either promote it to a blocking lane or fold its rows into the broad clinical benchmark with updated thresholds once it has a stable green baseline.

layers.weakness_found: Patient wording such as water pill, pee test, belly pain, sugar diabetes, heart tracing, scope, and blood thinner can return no result or rank literal/anatomy/noise concepts before the intended clinical concept.
layers.recommended_solution: Keep a reviewed consumer lay-language lane with expected CUIs and explicit literal-drift disallowed CUIs, then promote it to blocking only after the baseline is green.

layers.weakness_found: The current lane regressed to 11/12 top-on-target and lost expected PubMed/PMC evidence sources.
layers.recommended_solution: Fix current-versus-history ranking and evidence-source retention before release.

layers.weakness_found: The current approved PubMed suite is 3/13 with 44/60 expected concepts at top 10 and 2 wrong first results.
layers.recommended_solution: Fix long-document sectioning, evidence carry-forward, and secondary-concept ranking; this layer should turn green when the weakness is solved.

layers.weakness_found: Recall improvements can promote wrong concepts unless reviewed precision cases, known false positives, and disallowed IDs stay protected.
layers.recommended_solution: Keep the reviewed precision audit classified, keep useful extras protected, and require false-positive/source-quality gate tests before accepting recall changes.

layers.weakness_found: CUI2Vec and BioConceptVec can add useful related-concept recall, but they are distributional CUI-neighbor signals and can introduce low-value association drift.
layers.recommended_solution: Keep SapBERT as the active query/document vector baseline. Keep CUI2Vec and BioConceptVec in the loop as opt-in subset and ablation probes with explicit positive neighbors and disallowed default drift cases.

layers.weakness_found: A broad CUI benchmark can pass while a specific evidence source stops helping the query it is supposed to support.
layers.recommended_solution: Keep the routine source-specific lane focused on DailyMed, MedlinePlus, and PubMed/PMC. Treat ClinicalTrials.gov posted outcomes as opt-in only, and keep PubTator3 as a sampled relation-candidate stub unless validated against PubMed/PMC for a measured gap.

layers.weakness_found: The focused PubMed probe has improved from 1/7 to 3/7 but still misses required concepts.
layers.recommended_solution: Use this smaller lane for rapid tuning, then confirm every fix on the approved PubMed suite.
